reports.title: Facebook Report - 30/04/2026 to 30/05/2026
reports.summary: Over the period April 30 to May 30, 2026, the Facebook and Instagram accounts (primarily Instagram) achieved a total reach of 21,036 and 21,708 views, with 422 interactions, 492 clicks, and 96 new follows, resulting in a 2.01% engagement rate. The strongest performance came from early posts (around April 1–3), with reach peaking at 1,910 and 1,772, while recent posts (April 23–24) saw a sharp decline, indicating a need to refresh content strategy. The most important metric insight is that the engagement rate (2.01%) is below industry benchmarks, suggesting content resonance issues. The key opportunity is to replicate the high-reach pattern of top-performing posts from early April.
takeaways: Total reach of 21,036 and 21,708 views indicate solid visibility, but engagement rate of 2.01% lags behind the typical 3-5% benchmark for Instagram.
The top three posts (April 1-3) generated over 5,000 combined reach, while recent posts averaged under 100 reach, highlighting a significant content performance drop.
Clicks (492) outnumber interactions (422), suggesting that the audience is more action-oriented (e.g., link clicks) than engaged with likes/comments/shares.
New follows (96) are modest; conversion from reach to follow is only 0.46%, indicating a need for stronger call-to-action or follower incentives.
Instagram is the primary driver of all activity (100% of metrics), meaning Facebook presence is negligible or not tracked, which may require strategic review.
actions: Analyze the content and posting strategy of the top three posts (April 1-3) to identify themes, formats, or timings that drove high reach, and replicate them in upcoming posts.
Implement a content calendar that diversifies post types (e.g., video, carousel, stories) and increases posting frequency to revive declining reach and engagement.
Launch a targeted follow campaign with incentives (e.g., giveaways, exclusive content) to improve follow conversion rate from the current 0.46%.
Set up click-through tracking for all posts to better understand which content drives link clicks and optimize for higher conversion.
Review the Facebook page strategy; if no activity is planned, consider reallocating resources to Instagram or revising the platform focus.

reports.title: Instagram Report - 31/03/2026 to 29/04/2026
reports.summary: During the period from March 31, 2026 to April 29, 2026, the Instagram account achieved a total reach of 21,036 and 21,708 views, with 422 interactions, 492 clicks, and 96 new follows. The overall engagement rate was 2.01%. Content posted between April 19 and April 20 drove the highest reach and clicks, indicating strong audience resonance. However, the average interactions per post remain low, suggesting opportunities to enhance call-to-action strategies.
takeaways: Total reach of 21,036 and views of 21,708 indicate solid content distribution, but the 2.01% engagement rate signals room for deeper audience connection.
The top-performing post on April 19 achieved 5,475 reach and 77 clicks, highlighting the effectiveness of high-reach content in driving website visits.
Only 96 new follows were gained over 30 days, suggesting a need for follower growth strategies beyond organic reach.
Clicks peaked at 221 on April 20, while most posts had zero clicks, revealing inconsistent traffic generation across content.
Engagement (interactions) is heavily concentrated on a few posts, with many posts receiving fewer than 10 interactions, pointing to content quality or timing issues.
actions: Analyze top-performing posts from April 19-20 to identify common elements (e.g., format, hashtags, time of posting) and replicate successful patterns.
Implement a consistent posting schedule with at least 5 posts per week to maintain audience momentum and increase total reach.
Introduce interactive elements (e.g., polls, questions, quizzes) in stories and captions to boost interactions and engagement rate.
Launch a targeted growth campaign (e.g., collaborate with micro-influencers or run a giveaway) to accelerate follower acquisition beyond the current 96 per month.
Review posts with zero clicks to optimize call-to-action placement and link accessibility, aiming to increase click-through rate from under 2.5%.

reports.title: Instagram Report - 31/03/2026 to 29/04/2026
reports.summary: Instagram appears to be your best current opportunity based on interaction, click, and follow activity.
takeaways: Instagram appears to be your best current opportunity based on interaction, click, and follow activity.
actions: Instagram is currently the strongest-performing channel. It is recommended to replicate the format, structure, and posting approach behind its most successful content.
Engagement is responding well relative to reach. Review the strongest interaction days and repeat the topic, creative style, or timing that contributed to that performance.
Clicks are already being generated. Strengthening landing-page alignment and repeating the strongest call-to-action style should improve conversion quality.
